name: El Planerón by Bike
name_original: Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitológica de El Planerón desde Beclhite
description: This moderate 23.9-mile mountain biking route combines historical exploration of Pueblo Viejo de Belchite with the natural beauty of El Plan
region_intro: Mountain biking around Belchite offers routes through a diverse landscape characterized by the Belchitana Steppe, the Aguasvivas River, and the Hoces del Campo de Belchite gorges. The terrain includes wide horizons, extensive flatlands, and traditional olive groves, contrasting with rugged gullies and canyons. Riders can explore historical sites like the Pueblo Viejo de Belchite, which are often integrated into the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Belchite The most popular mountain bike trail is El Planerón by Bike, a 23.9 miles (38.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route traverses the unique steppe landscape of El Planerón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Old Village of Belchite – Pico Sillero loop from Belchite, a difficult 42.0 miles (67.7 km) path. This route offers views of the historical Old Village of Belchite and extends towards Pico Sillero. Local mountain bikers also love the Alborge – Meanders Viewpoint loop from Alborge, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Belchite is defined by the open Belchitana Steppe, the riverine landscapes of the Aguasvivas River, and the dramatic canyons of the Hoces del Campo de Belchite. The network of more than 20 mountain bike trails offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Belchite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.75 stars from a few reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Belchite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Belchite?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Belchite, offering a diverse range of experiences through the region's unique landscapes. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Belchite?
A: Belchite's mountain biking terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter the open, 'almost desert-like' Belchitana Steppe with wide horizons and traditional olive groves, as well as the riverine landscapes of the Aguasvivas River. The dramatic canyons and ravines of the Hoces del Campo de Belchite also offer rugged gullies and unique microclimates.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Belchite?
A: Yes, many routes around Belchite are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Pico Sillero loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 42-mile route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19813432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alborge – Meanders Viewpoint loop from Alborge&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 21.3-mile ride through varied terrain.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can integrate historical and natural sights into your rides. The poignant ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite&lt;/a&gt; are a significant historical monument. Natural features include the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros&lt;/a&gt; along the Aguasvivas River, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient engineering marvel.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Belchite?
A: The Belchitana Steppe transforms with vibrant blooms in spring, offering a captivating visual experience. This season, along with autumn, generally provides pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot due to the 'almost desert-like' conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Belchite?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region offers options for various skill levels. For families, it's best to look for shorter, less technical sections or trails with minimal elevation gain. Always check the specific route details on komoot for difficulty ratings and terrain descriptions to ensure it suits your family's experience level.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Belchite?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.75 stars. Riders often praise the unique and diverse landscapes, from the wide-open steppe to the dramatic gorges, and the opportunity to combine outdoor adventure with exploring historical sites like the Pueblo Viejo de Belchite.

Q: Are there any protected natural areas I can explore by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region includes protected areas like the Wild Fauna Shelter 'La Lomaza' and the Birds Reserve '&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Planerón&lt;/a&gt;'. These areas are vital for ornithological tourism and protect steppe bird species. While some areas may have specific access rules, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988084952/el-planeron-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Planerón by Bike&lt;/a&gt; traverse the unique steppe landscape of El Planerón, allowing you to experience these natural environments.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in Belchite?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31886956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 20 miles (32 km) and takes around 2 hours 40 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes can extend to over 40 miles and require several hours to complete, such as the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Pico Sillero loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Belchite?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Belchite start from the town itself or nearby villages. It's generally advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking information. Parking is typically available in or near the main settlements that serve as trailheads.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Belchite?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in Belchite are rated moderate, with some difficult options. While there are no trails explicitly rated 'easy' in the guide data, beginners can look for shorter, flatter sections of moderate trails or explore less technical paths around the villages. Always review the route's elevation profile and surface description before heading out.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Belchite and the surrounding villages offer local amenities where you can find cafes and places to eat. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through or end near these settlements, especially for longer rides, to refuel and relax. Always check opening hours, particularly outside of peak season.
tips: At this point we find ourselves in front of the largest oasis of the Belchite steppes. We will find an information panel that will give us more information about its formation and the birds that we can observe

We are located in the Ornithological Reserve of El Planerón, a place with a special charm, it does not seem that we are so close to Zaragoza.
It is also home to countless steppe birds and other rare birds, the observation of which is somewhat complicated in the summer months due to the lack of water.
It is very well indicated.

We are in the old town of Belchite, we will walk the streets of the Old Town knowing the events that took place during the days of the Battle of Belchite. We cannot miss it if we want to know our history.
Visit the website for schedules, prices and guided tours: belchite.es/visita-guiada-pueblo-viejo-diurna

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from Belchite, where you can find parking facilities. The trails are generally well-signposted from the town.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the area, the El Planerón Ornithological Reserve is a protected natural area. It's best to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially the ground-nesting birds, and to check for any specific regulations regarding pets within the reserve.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. While the terrain can be ridden year-round, be aware that conditions can become muddy during winter and early spring due to rainfall.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Reserva Ornitológica de El Planerón?
faqs.answer: Access to the El Planerón Ornithological Reserve for mountain biking does not typically require a specific permit or entrance fee. However, as it is a protected area, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and follow any posted regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily a mix of gravel, asphalt, and natural paths, characterized by sandy and dry conditions typical of a steppe ecosystem. It is generally flat and open, with minimal elevation gain, making it accessible for most riders. Some sections may also pass through olive groves.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a unique combination of historical and natural sights. You'll pass through the haunting ruins of the Old Village of Belchite, offering a poignant historical experience. You'll also explore the El Planerón Ornithological Reserve, known for its exceptional steppe landscapes and birdwatching opportunities, and the Balsa El Planerón.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The El Planerón Ornithological Reserve is renowned for its steppe birds, including the rare DuPont's lark. You might also spot various birds of prey such as the Golden Eagle, Montagu's Harrier, and Common Kestrel, along with other specialized flora and fauna adapted to this arid environment.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered suitable for beginners and families. It features accessible terrain, relatively low technical difficulty, and modest elevation gain, making it a pleasant experience for a wide range of cyclists. It's ideal for gravel bikes or mountain bikes (BTT).

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other named trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other named paths, including the Camino del Maestrazgo-Bajo Aragón, Camino Balsa Paneron, Calle Mayor, and Calle del Señor.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Belchite, the starting point for this route, offers various amenities. You can find places to eat, cafes, and potentially accommodation options in the town. The El Planerón Ornithological Reserve also has an interpretation center and a bird observatory for visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any specific seasonal considerations for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally accessible, riders should be aware that the terrain can become quite muddy during the winter and spring months, especially after rainfall. This might affect ride comfort and require more robust tires.
